contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 120
- Description: Sell-through inventory CI fix + truesight.me prod recovery + org domain verification + footgun postmortem
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/127
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_prod/pull/10
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/156
  
  Details:
  Two adjacent infrastructure fixes plus the footgun postmortem:
  
  1. Sell-through report inventory_units was 0 across all 17 partners. Diagnosed: scripts/sync_sell_through_report.py resolved partners-inventory.json via `_REPO.parent / "agroverse-inventory"` which is correct locally (sibling under ~/Applications/) but wrong in CI (actions/checkout puts agroverse-inventory INSIDE the workspace, not as a parent sibling). The bare except swallowed the FileNotFoundError, the function returned {}, and every cur_inv lookup defaulted to 0.0 silently. Fix: try both layouts in order, warn loudly on stderr when neither exists. Manually triggered the publish-partners-velocity workflow after merge; verified the regenerated file now shows real per-partner inventory (sacred-earth-farms: 19, hacker-dojo: 5, green-gulch: 18, total_inventory_units: 128.0 across the cohort).
  
  2. truesight.me went down briefly. Root cause was self-inflicted: I had been using `gh repo sync TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_prod --source TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta --force` after each beta merge. The --force overwrote truesight_me_prod's CNAME file (truesight.me) with beta's (beta.truesight.me). GitHub Pages released the truesight.me claim. A dormant unrelated GitHub Pages site ("lisa") that had truesight.me in its CNAME file snapped up the claim. Recovery:
     a. Restored CNAME on truesight_me_prod via the GitHub Contents API.
     b. Shipped self-healing guardrail workflow at truesight_me_prod/.github/workflows/protect-cname.yml that auto-restores CNAME to truesight.me on every push to main if drift is detected.
     c. Performed org-level domain verification (the durable fix). Added _github-pages-challenge-TrueSightDAO TXT records to Route53 (cypher_def-managed AWS account, autopilot IAM identity) for BOTH truesight.me and agroverse.shop hosted zones. Verified DNS propagation via Google Public DNS. Operator clicked Verify; GitHub released the rogue claim; the prod repo's Pages site re-claimed the domain. HTTPS certificate re-approved for both apex and www.
     d. Triggered a no-op commit on truesight_me_prod to force a fresh Pages rebuild and Fastly edge invalidation while the operator was seeing a stale 404 from the prior takeover.
  
  3. Footgun postmortem captured in agentic_ai_context. Added a new section to LLM_DISCOVERY_SURFACE.md titled "Footgun: DO NOT gh repo sync --force to truesight_me_prod" with rule, rationale, the guardrail workflow pointer, and the analogous risk on agroverse_shop_prod ← agroverse_shop_beta. Appended a CONTEXT_UPDATES.md entry so future LLM sessions reading the change-log see the incident arc.
  
  Org-level verification means GitHub now refuses any non-TrueSightDAO repo trying to claim truesight.me or agroverse.shop. The class of subdomain-takeover that took the site down today cannot recur on either domain.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 200.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------
